title: Water Leak Sensor Zone Correlation
spl:
index=environment sourcetype="leak_detection"
| where leak_detected=1
| stats count by zone, crac_id
| sort -count
description: Correlates multiple rope sensors to localize leak source and trigger EPO workflows under raised floor.
value: Correlates multiple rope sensors to localize leak source and trigger EPO workflows under raised floor.
implementation: On any leak, show adjacent zones and nearest CRAC/isolation valve. Integrate with facilities runbook.
visualization: Floor plan (zones), Table (active leaks), Single value (leak count).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Leak detection panel ‚Üí Splunk.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="leak_detection"` (zone, sensor_id, conductivity).
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
On any leak, show adjacent zones and nearest CRAC/isolation valve. Integrate with facilities runbook.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=environment sourcetype="leak_detection"
| where leak_detected=1
| stats count by zone, crac_id
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Water Leak Sensor Zone Correlation** ‚Äî Correlates multiple rope sensors to localize leak source and trigger EPO workflows under raised floor.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="leak_detection"` (zone, sensor_id, conductivity). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Leak detection panel ‚Üí Splunk. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: environment; **sourcetype**: leak_detection. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=environment, sourcetype="leak_detection". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where leak_detected=1` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by zone, crac_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Floor plan (zones), Table (active leaks), Single value (leak count).
grandmaExplanation: Correlates multiple rope sensors to localize leak source and trigger EPO workflows under raised flo ‚Äî so power, cooling, and physical access problems are found before they hurt uptime.
